question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/GrpcApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: retrieve(TopicMessageFilter, boolean) in PollingTopicMessageRetriever] Can an unprivileged external user subscribe with a startTime far in the past, causing the throttled retriever to poll indefinitely (numRepeats=Long.MAX_VALUE) and the timeout of 60 seconds to fire, terminating the historical retrieval early and exporting only a partial set of records?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/GrpcApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: isComplete() in PollingContext of PollingTopicMessageRetriever] Can an unprivileged external user subscribe with a limit exactly equal to maxPageSize, causing isComplete() to return false (pageSize.get() < maxPageSize is false only when equal), resulting in an extra unnecessary poll that may return zero records but still delays or disrupts the export stream?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/GrpcApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: isComplete() in PollingContext of PollingTopicMessageRetriever] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the unthrottled retriever path (throttled=false) where isComplete() only checks limitHit and never checks page size, causing the retriever to keep polling even after all messages are returned, potentially exporting duplicate records if the database is updated between polls?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/GrpcApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findByFilter(TopicMessageFilter) in TopicMessageRepositoryCustomImpl] Can an unprivileged external user submit a ConsensusTopicQuery with limit=1, causing the TOPIC_MESSAGES_BY_ID_QUERY_HINT (set local random_page_cost = 0) to be skipped, which changes the PostgreSQL query planner's index selection and causes the query to use a suboptimal plan that returns incorrect or incomplete results?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/GrpcApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findByFilter(TopicMessageFilter) in TopicMessageRepositoryCustomImpl] Can an unprivileged external user submit a ConsensusTopicQuery with no endTime, causing the findByFilter() query to omit the upper bound predicate and return all messages from startTime to the present, potentially exporting more records than expected or causing memory exhaustion that truncates the result set?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/GrpcApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findByFilter(TopicMessageFilter) in TopicMessageRepositoryCustomImpl] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that findByFilter() calls getResultList().stream() instead of getResultStream(), causing the entire result set to be loaded into memory at once, and if the result set is large enough to cause an OOM error, the stream terminates early and exports only partial records?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/GrpcApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: onNext(TopicMessage) in TopicContext] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a race condition in the non-atomic sequence of last.set(topicMessage) and count.incrementAndGet() in TopicContext.onNext(), where a concurrent thread reads an inconsistent state of last and count, causing isNext() to incorrectly throw an IllegalStateException and terminate the stream with missing records?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/GrpcApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: missingMessages(TopicContext, TopicMessage)] Can an unprivileged external user subscribe to a topic where the importer is delayed, causing the safety check to trigger missingMessages() with current=null, and the gapFilter is built without an endTime, causing the retriever to fetch all messages from startTime to present and potentially export duplicate records when the live stream also delivers the same messages?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/GrpcApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: subscribeTopic(TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged external user set limit=0 in ConsensusTopicQuery, which maps to hasLimit()=false (since limit > 0 is false), causing the subscription to be unbounded and never terminate, potentially causing resource exhaustion that affects the export of records to other subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/GrpcApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: toFilter(ConsensusTopicQuery) in ConsensusController] Can an unprivileged external user omit both consensusStartTime and consensusEndTime from ConsensusTopicQuery, causing the filter to use the default startTime=DomainUtils.now(), which means all historical messages before the subscription time are skipped and never exported?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/GrpcApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: toResponse(TopicMessage)] Can an unprivileged external user subscribe to a topic where t.getMessage() is null, causing ProtoUtil.toByteString(null) to return ByteString.EMPTY, resulting in an exported ConsensusTopicResponse with an empty message field that misrepresents the actual stored record?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/GrpcApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: toResponse(TopicMessage)] Can an unprivileged external user subscribe to a topic where t.getRunningHash() is null, causing ProtoUtil.toByteString(null) to return ByteString.EMPTY, resulting in an exported ConsensusTopicResponse with an empty runningHash that makes the record appear invalid or corrupted?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/GrpcApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: toResponse(TopicMessage)] Can an unprivileged external user subscribe to a topic where t.getConsensusTimestamp() is null, causing ProtoUtil.toTimestamp(null) to return null, and the setConsensusTimestamp(null) call to throw a NullPointerException that terminates the stream and causes all subsequent records to be missing from the export?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/GrpcApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: toResponse(TopicMessage)] Can an unprivileged external user subscribe to a chunked topic message where chunkNum is set but chunkTotal is null, causing chunkBuilder.setTotal(null) to throw a NullPointerException that terminates the stream and causes all subsequent records to be missing from the export?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/GrpcApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getNodes(AddressBookQuery) in NetworkController] Can an unprivileged external user submit an AddressBookQuery with limit=0, causing NetworkServiceImpl.getNodes() to use Long.MAX_VALUE as the take limit, streaming the entire address book and potentially causing memory exhaustion that truncates the exported node records?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/GrpcApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: toFilter(AddressBookQuery) in NetworkController] Can an unprivileged external user omit the fileId field from AddressBookQuery, causing filter.fileId to be null and getValidFileIds().contains(null) to return false, throwing an IllegalArgumentException that prevents any address book records from being exported?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/GrpcApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getNodes(AddressBookFilter) in NetworkServiceImpl] Can an unprivileged external user submit an AddressBookQuery with a valid fileId but for which no address book exists in the database, causing addressBookRepository.findLatestTimestamp() to throw EntityNotFoundException and export zero node records, even if a valid address book exists under a different file ID?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/GrpcApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: page(AddressBookContext) in NetworkServiceImpl] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a scenario where nodeStakeRepository.findAllStakeByConsensusTimestamp() returns an empty map, causing all exported NodeAddress records to have stake=0 (overriding the actual stake), resulting in incorrect stake data being exported to the subscriber?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/GrpcApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: toNodeAddress(AddressBookEntry) in NetworkController] Can an unprivileged external user subscribe to the address book and receive a NodeAddress where toIpAddressV4() silently returns ByteString.EMPTY due to an UnknownHostException, causing the exported record to have a missing IP address field that misrepresents the actual node endpoint?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/GrpcApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: toIpAddressV4(String) in NetworkController] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a scenario where a stored IP address in the address book is a hostname (not a numeric IP), causing InetAddress.getByName() to perform a DNS lookup, and if DNS resolution fails, the exported NodeAddress has an empty ipAddressV4 field, resulting in an incorrect record?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/GrpcApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: toStatusRuntimeException(Throwable) in ProtoUtil] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a TransientDataAccessException during a topic message query, causing toStatusRuntimeException() to return RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED with a generic DB_ERROR message, which causes the client to retry and potentially miss records that were available before the transient error?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/GrpcApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: toStatusRuntimeException(Throwable) in ProtoUtil] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a NonTransientDataAccessResourceException by exhausting database connections, causing toStatusRuntimeException() to return UNAVAILABLE, which terminates the stream and causes all pending records to be missing from the export without any recovery mechanism?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/GrpcApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: subscribeTopic(ConsensusTopicQuery, StreamObserver) in ConsensusController] Can an unprivileged external user cancel a gRPC subscription (triggering disposable.dispose()) at the exact moment when missingMessages() is executing a gap recovery query, causing the gap recovery to be interrupted and the missing messages to never be exported to the subscriber?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/GrpcApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: incomingMessages(TopicContext)] Can an unprivileged external user subscribe to a topic with a startTime in the past, and because incomingMessages() sets startTime = last.getConsensusTimestamp() + 1 (or filter.getStartTime() if last is null), a race condition between historical retrieval completing and the live listener starting causes a gap window where messages are missed?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/GrpcApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: subscribeTopic(TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the filter() predicate that checks t.getSequenceNumber() > last.getSequenceNumber() to cause a message with a valid but non-sequential sequence number to be silently dropped, resulting in a missing record in the exported stream?
